sites.notes: Raw store.Get loads session bead from city store. Can flip to session.Store.Get once SessionStore wrapper is available.
Read session template metadata. Direct conversion to Info.Template field.
Read session common name metadata. Direct conversion to Info.CommonName field.
Raw store.Get loads session bead from city store for provider session key persistence. Can flip to session.Store.Get.

Passes whole session bead to helper that cracks metadata. sessionProviderFamily(beads.Bead) calls ProviderFamilyFromMetadata which reads metadata[builtin_ancestor], metadata[provider_kind], metadata[provider]. Info-form sibling would be ProviderFamily(info Info) that extracts provider and calls ProviderFamilyFromMetadata(map{provider:info.Provider}, "").
Read session_key metadata for idempotency check (write guard). Direct conversion to Info.SessionKey field.

files.recommendation: All 6 raw session reads are convertible once sessionProviderFamily gets an Info-form sibling. Five reads (template, common_name, session_key, both store.Get calls) are straightforward metadata reads or store.Get calls. The sessionProviderFamily call at line 581 requires a ProviderFamily(info Info) method wrapper around the existing ProviderFamilyFromMetadata metadata-cracking logic.

sites.notes: Raw bead load from beads.Store. The store parameter is passed through resolveSessionIDAllowClosedWithConfig context which confirms session-class. All subsequent .Metadata accesses on this b derive from this read.
LegacyWorkDirMetadataKey='work_dir'. This field is mirrored on Info.WorkDir. Value is trimmed; Info.WorkDir carries raw untrimmed value from bead.Metadata.
provider_kind is a legacy fallback key. Primary key is 'provider' (Info.Provider). This read implements a fallback: if provider_kind is empty, line 195 reads 'provider' instead.
Fallback read after checking provider_kind is empty. Info.Provider directly mirrors b.Metadata['provider']. Value is trimmed here; Info carries raw value.
Info.SessionKey directly mirrors b.Metadata['session_key']. Value is trimmed here; Info carries raw value.
Reading b.CreatedAt timestamp. Info.CreatedAt mirrors b.CreatedAt directly.

Guard check: IsSessionBeadOrRepairable inspects bead internals (Type, Labels). Beads failing this check are skipped, so downstream logic only operates on session beads. b comes from sessionLogFallbackCandidates which returns beads from store.ListByMetadata.

Same key as line 189. Comparing raw bead metadata work_dir against extracted logCtx.workDir (which was itself extracted from an Info field). Trim-based compare means values must be identical after trim.
Same as line 193: legacy fallback key with no Info field. Primary is 'provider'.
Same as line 195: fallback read for provider. Trim-based compare with logCtx.provider.
Reading 'state' metadata. Info.MetadataState carries raw state value. This code reads the raw metadata directly (not normalized). b comes from sessionLogFallbackCandidateLive parameter.

files.recommendation: All 9 session metadata sites are convertible to session.Info reads. No helper functions need Info sibling methods; IsSessionBeadOrRepairable is a guard that filters beads, not a metadata-cracking helper. The file can move to fully typed session.Info API after confirming that Info.WorkDir, Info.Provider, Info.SessionKey, Info.MetadataState, and Info.CreatedAt match the codec projection (they do per info_store.go).

sites.notes: Raw store.Get() loads a session bead (verified by IsSessionBeadOrRepairable guard on line 67). Bead flows into multiple helpers that crack raw metadata. This is the session-bead entry point.

Bead passed to helper that cracks raw metadata (line 134).

Raw Metadata read for 'template' in normalizedSessionTemplate. Maps to Info.Template. This helper is called from sessionWakeHasRunnableTemplate and has no Info-form sibling.
Bead passed to helper that cracks raw metadata (agent_name and labels).

Raw Labels access in sessionBeadAgentName scanning for agent:<name> prefix. Maps to Info.Labels which projects verbatim.

sites.notes: Session bead (guarded by line 49: session.IsSessionBeadOrRepairable). Reads alias field and trims whitespace before comparison; conversion maintains semantics.

Session bead. AliasHistory helper reconstructs from alias_history metadata key; Info.AliasHistory already carries the parsed result. Helper has Info-form sibling in session/alias.go (projecting the same key-value pair).

sites.code: if bead, getErr := store.Get(id); getErr == nil && isNamedSessionBead(bead) {
sites.notes: store.Get(id) loads a session bead by ID. The bead is immediately passed to isNamedSessionBead helper which reads .Metadata[configured_named_session]. The Info struct carries this as ConfiguredNamedSession field (projected identically via strings.TrimSpace(i.ConfiguredNamedSession) == 'true' equivalence in named_config.go IsNamedSessionInfo).

namedSessionIdentity reads .Metadata[configured_named_identity] from the same bead loaded at line 147. Info.ConfiguredNamedIdentity carries the raw (untrimmed) value; NamedSessionIdentityInfo applies strings.TrimSpace for comparison. Helper namedSessionIdentity(bead) wraps session.NamedSessionIdentity(bead).

session.ListAllSessionBeads(store) returns raw session beads from the store. The beads are then iterated and their metadata/labels are read in lines 197, 211. ListAllSessionBeads internally filters via IsSessionBeadOrRepairable (per line 192 comment), confirming these are session beads. This is a bulk load operation where beads flow into subsequent metadata reads.

Reads alias metadata key from session beads returned by ListAllSessionBeads. The bead source is confirmed as session beads by ListAllSessionBeads and the loop comment on line 192. Info.Alias carries this field (line 42 of info_store.go). Used for qualified alias basename matching.

Reads alias metadata from a match bead in the error formatting path. Same source as line 197 (beads from ListAllSessionBeads iteration). Info.Alias carries this field. This is a read-only informational access for error message construction.

files.recommendation: All five sites are session-bead reads that can be converted to session.Info once Info-form siblings of isNamedSessionBead, namedSessionIdentity (or inline Info.ConfiguredNamedSession/ConfiguredNamedIdentity), and a Store.List() or Store.ListOpen() wrapper of ListAllSessionBeads are built. The helper resolveOpenQualifiedAliasBasename itself requires conversion once Store.List routes its reads through the Info projection. No blockers; all metadata keys are already mirrored on Info struct.

slept_at is not exposed on session.Info; it is internal lifecycle timing metadata. Remains on raw bead but can be accessed via session bead after migration.

ResolveRunID is a helper that reads raw metadata keys (workflow_id, molecule_id, gc.root_bead_id) to find the run root. No Info-form sibling exists; this is a generic bead utility not session-specific. Session context available but run-id resolution is orthogonal to session identity.

files.recommendation: File is fully convertible to session.Info. All session beads come from sessionBeadSnapshot (guaranteed session class). Two straightforward Info fields (SessionName, State) can be flipped immediately. beadmeta.ResolveRunID is a generic cross-bead utility and does not need session-Info sibling; it already works with metadata map generically. Usage-fact timing keys (awake_started_at, slept_at, usage_compute_emitted_at) and active-work-bead pointer are internal metadata markers not on Info; they remain on raw beads but access pattern stays read-only compatible with session.Info wrapper.

Callers that pass the resolved string result to helpers like findAgentByTemplate and sessionBeadTemplate do not require Info-form sibling helpers; they only consume the string value, not the full bead or Info struct.
